svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: var(--ge-adaptive-bg, #ffffff); color-scheme: light dark;

<mxfile host="app.diagrams.net" agent="Claude" pages="5" scale="1" border="0"> <diagram name="AUTOSAR - OSI Mapping" id="autosar-osi-mapping"> <mxGraphModel dx="1280" dy="2134" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1" tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1" page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="1000" pageHeight="600" math="0" shadow="0"> <root> <mxCell id="0" /> <mxCell id="1" parent="0" /> <mxCell id="o101" parent="1" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;fontStyle=1;" value="UDS / AUTOSAR Communication Stack Mapped onto the OSI Reference Model" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="1000" y="10" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o102" parent="1" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontSize=12;fontColor=#555555;fontStyle=1;" value="OSI Layer (ISO 7498-1)" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="20" width="200" x="60" y="52" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o103" parent="1" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontSize=12;fontColor=#555555;fontStyle=1;" value="AUTOSAR / UDS module implementing it" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="20" width="460" x="300" y="52" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o104" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#d9ecff;strokeColor=#1f5fa8;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#0d3a6b;" value="Layer 7 Application" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="200" x="60" y="78" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o105" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#d9ecff;strokeColor=#1f5fa8;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#0d3a6b;" value="UDS (ISO 14229-1) service definitions, executed by DCM (DSL / DSD / DSP) — confirmed by ISO doc: "Unified diagnostic services occupy layer 7"" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="460" x="300" y="78" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o106" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;strokeColor=#999999;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;dashed=1;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#777777;" value="Layer 6 Presentation" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="52" width="200" x="60" y="154" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o107" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;strokeColor=#999999;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;dashed=1;fontColor=#777777;" value="Not separately implemented in the CAN diagnostic stack — no dedicated AUTOSAR module" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="52" width="460" x="300" y="154" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o108" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;strokeColor=#999999;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;dashed=1;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#777777;" value="Layer 5 Session" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="52" width="200" x="60" y="218" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o109" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;strokeColor=#999999;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;dashed=1;fontColor=#777777;" value="Not separately implemented — diagnostic "session" concept (defaultSession, programmingSession, etc.) is actually a Layer-7 UDS concept (DiagnosticSessionControl), not an OSI L5 session" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="52" width="460" x="300" y="218" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o110" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff3d6;strokeColor=#c99a2e;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#7a5c10;" value="Layer 4 Transport" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="200" x="60" y="282" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o111" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff3d6;strokeColor=#c99a2e;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#7a5c10;" value="CanTp — segmentation/reassembly, flow control (implements ISO 15765-2, which is itself titled "Transport protocol AND network layer services")" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="460" x="300" y="282" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o112" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff3d6;strokeColor=#c99a2e;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#7a5c10;" value="Layer 3 Network" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="200" x="60" y="358" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o113" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff3d6;strokeColor=#c99a2e;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#7a5c10;" value="Also CanTp (addressing/N_AI per ISO 15765-2). PduR does NOT belong here — see note at right: it is a routing/multiplexing module, not an OSI layer" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="460" x="300" y="358" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o114" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e6f5e6;strokeColor=#3a8a3a;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#255a25;" value="Layer 2 Data Link" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="200" x="60" y="434" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o115" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e6f5e6;strokeColor=#3a8a3a;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#255a25;" value="CanIf — CAN frame handling, ID filtering/routing between CanDrv and upper modules (CanTp / COM / PduR)" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="460" x="300" y="434" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o116" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#eeeeee;strokeColor=#666666;fontSize=12;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontStyle=1;fontColor=#000000;" value="Layer 1 Physical" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="200" x="60" y="510" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o117" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#eeeeee;strokeColor=#666666;fontSize=11;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#000000;" value="CanDrv (CAN controller driver) + CAN transceiver / bus wiring — the actual physical medium and its closest software driver" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="64" width="460" x="300" y="510" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o118" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff8e1;strokeColor=#b8860b;fontSize=10.5;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;dashed=1;fontColor=#7a5c10;" value="PduR (PDU Router) Routes/multiplexes PDUs between DCM, CanTp, COM, and other upper/lower BSW modules. It has NO formal OSI-layer equivalent — it is an AUTOSAR-internal software dispatcher, not addressing/routing across a network the way an OSI Layer-3 protocol does." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="200" x="790" y="282" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="o119" edge="1" parent="1" style="endArrow=block;html=1;strokeColor=#888888;strokeWidth=1.3;fillColor=#888888;"> div.div: PduR (PDU Router) Routes/multiplexes PDUs between DCM, CanTp, COM, and other upper/lower BSW modules. It has NO formal OSI-layer equivalent — it is an AUTOSAR-internal software dispatcher, not addressing/routing across a network the way an OSI Layer-3 protocol does.
